blocks.speaker: DeShawn
blocks.content: See that's what I'm saying. You already tracked it.

blocks.speaker: DeShawn
blocks.content: Fair. Most people don't see it at all.

blocks.speaker: DeShawn
blocks.content: Man I've been at it since 7:55 I already told you.

DeShawn walked with me to the bus stop. He talked most of the way — not nervously, just constantly, like he processed by narrating. By the time his bus came he'd said something smart about every person we'd talked about today.

blocks.speaker: DeShawn
blocks.content: Aye — Lincoln always this much, or is this a first-day thing?

blocks.speaker: DeShawn
blocks.content: Right. Me too. Okay. Research ongoing.
He got on the bus. I kept walking.
Fourteen more blocks. The neighborhood settled around me. School noise faded and it was just cars and kids on stoops and the street knowing nothing had changed for it.
I thought about the girl in the hall. The one I didn't know the name of.
Then I thought about Daniel. Then Jonathan's table. Then how Maya asked questions like she expected real answers.

blocks.prompt: Fourteen blocks. What carries the walk?

options.label: Daniel. What you said — or what you didn't.
Jonathan. He fit so fast. You're still finding your footing.
